summary: Iteration 16 testing complete. All 24 multi-camera backend tests pass (100%). Frontend Camera tab shows SPLIT view with Primary (VO) and Thermal panels. View switcher buttons (SPLIT, VO ONLY, THERMAL) work correctly. Thermal CAPTURE button triggers on-demand capture and displays false-color thermal image with iron palette. Dashboard sidebar shows CAMERAS section listing both Forward (VO) and Thermal (Down) cameras.

issues: Minor console warnings about chart dimensions - not blocking functionality (same as iteration 15)

passed_tests: GET /api/cameras returns 200 with array of 2 camera slots
GET /api/cameras includes PRIMARY slot with correct metadata (Forward VO, has_vo=true)
GET /api/cameras includes SECONDARY slot with correct metadata (USB_THERMAL, 256x192, has_vo=false)
GET /api/camera/secondary/stats returns 200 with thermal camera stats
GET /api/camera/secondary/stats returns slot=SECONDARY, camera_type=USB_THERMAL
POST /api/camera/secondary/capture returns 200 with success=true
POST /api/camera/secondary/capture increments frame_count
POST /api/camera/secondary/capture sets active=true flag
GET /api/camera/secondary/frame returns 200 with valid PNG image after capture
GET /api/camera/secondary/frame includes X-Frame-Id header
GET /api/camera still works (backward compatibility)
GET /api/camera/frame still returns primary camera frame (backward compatibility)
Both cameras can be queried simultaneously
Full thermal capture workflow: capture -> stats -> frame works end-to-end
Primary VO still works when secondary thermal is active
Frontend Camera tab shows MULTI-CAMERA header with '2 / 2 Active' badge
Frontend Camera tab view switcher has SPLIT, VO ONLY, THERMAL buttons
Frontend SPLIT view shows Primary (VO) panel on left, Thermal panel on right
Frontend VO ONLY view shows full-width Primary camera panel
Frontend THERMAL view shows full-width Thermal panel
Frontend ThermalPanel has CAPTURE button that triggers capture
Frontend ThermalPanel has AUTO (1FPS) button for auto-refresh
Frontend ThermalPanel displays false-color thermal image with iron palette
Frontend Dashboard sidebar CAMERAS section shows Forward (VO) and Thermal (Down)
critical_code_review_comments: server.py /api/cameras endpoint correctly returns both PRIMARY and SECONDARY camera slots
server.py /api/camera/secondary/* endpoints properly delegate to runtime.get_secondary_camera_stats(), capture_secondary(), get_secondary_frame_data()
native_bridge.py __init_multicam() lazy-initializes secondary camera state for C++ runtime compatibility
native_bridge.py get_secondary_frame_data() generates simulated thermal frame with moving hotspots
simulator.py JTZeroSimulator has full multi-camera support with _secondary_camera state
ThermalPanel.js applies iron palette false-color to grayscale thermal data
App.js CameraTab correctly passes cameras array to ThermalPanel via secondaryCam prop
App.js DashboardTab sidebar CAMERAS section iterates over cameras array

success_rate.frontend: 100% - All UI components render and interact correctly

context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 16 verified multi-camera system for JT-Zero drone: 1) GET /api/cameras returns array with PRIMARY (CSI Forward VO) and SECONDARY (USB Thermal) slots. 2) Secondary camera endpoints: GET /api/camera/secondary/stats, POST /api/camera/secondary/capture, GET /api/camera/secondary/frame. 3) Thermal camera is on-demand capture (not continuous) to save Pi Zero 2W resources. 4) Frontend Camera tab has view switcher (SPLIT/VO ONLY/THERMAL) and ThermalPanel with CAPTURE and AUTO buttons. 5) Thermal frames rendered with iron palette false-color. 6) Dashboard sidebar shows both cameras. 7) MOCKED: Secondary thermal camera generates simulated thermal frames with moving hotspots (no physical USB thermal camera in preview environment). Total 24 new tests pass.

mocked_apis.note: Secondary thermal camera is SIMULATED - generates thermal hotspot patterns since no physical USB thermal camera in preview environment
mocked_endpoints: GET /api/camera/secondary/frame - Returns simulated 256x192 thermal frame with moving hotspots
native_bridge.py get_secondary_frame_data() - Generates synthetic thermal data

api_cameras.returns: Array of camera objects with slot, camera_type, camera_open, active, frame_count, fps_actual, width, height, label, has_vo

secondary_camera_apis.capture: POST /api/camera/secondary/capture - Triggers on-demand capture, returns {success: boolean}
secondary_camera_apis.frame: GET /api/camera/secondary/frame - Returns PNG image with X-Frame-Id header
